欢迎收看火箭报道3.19版！本周，我们很高兴地看到，由心宿二号和猎鹰9号运载火箭领衔的美国火箭在由于技术和天气问题进行了一系列擦洗后回到了正轨。与此同时，世界上的下一次发射是一次盛大的发射：下周，联盟号载着三名宇航员前往国际空间站。
一如既往，我们欢迎读者提交，如果您不想错过任何一期，请使用下面的框进行订阅(表单将不会显示在启用AMP的网站版本上)。每一份报告都将包括关于小型、中型和重型火箭的信息，以及日历上未来三次发射的快速展望。
维珍轨道公司的火箭价格不菲。随着维珍轨道公司(Virgin Orbit)在2020年底进行第二次发射尝试，其费用正在增加。维珍轨道公司由理查德·布兰森爵士于2011年创立，是他的维珍银河太空业务的一个分支，到目前为止还没有透露它花了多少钱。但业内官员估计，它已经花费了5亿至7亿美元来开发LauncherOne和支持它的基础设施。
一枚相对较小的火箭花了这么多钱。在一个新的特写中，ARS探索了这种情况是如何发生的，并调查了这家总部位于加利福尼亚州长滩的公司是否有盈利的途径。火箭实验室的竞争对手彼得·贝克对此表示怀疑。他在一次采访中表示：我看不出这个商业案例是如何结案的。你怎么花那么多钱并获得投资回报呢？此外，如果你已经花了那么多钱，而且你现在的处境，也许是时候重新考虑一下了。
贝克警告说，空间正变得过于拥挤。首席执行官贝克(我们知道，他在本周的报告中占据了重要位置)表示，目前太空中物体的绝对数量-在一定程度上要归功于SpaceX的卫星互联网星座Starlink-正在迅速增长，这使得为火箭发射新卫星找到一条明确的路径变得更加困难。他在接受CNN商业采访时表示，这对发射方面产生了巨大的影响。火箭必须设法在这些星座之间穿梭而上。
需要承担责任..。贝克说，总的来说，他并不反对SpaceX的计划或大规模的卫星星座。他指出，一旦投入使用，StarLink可以为数十亿仍然缺乏足够连接的人提供互联网接入，从而为地球上的生命带来巨大的好处。但是贝克说，他很担心他所看到的太空交通对他自己生意的影响有多快。他誓言要研究这一问题，以帮助推进关于负责任的空间交通管理的讨论。
跟上埃里克·伯杰的空间报道最简单的方法是注册他的时事通讯，我们会在你的收件箱里收集他的故事。
Wallops准备好增长了吗？沃洛普斯飞行设施在第二次世界大战期间作为海军航空站诞生，已经发射了超过1.6万枚火箭，其中大多数是用于科学研究的小型探空车。但是据华盛顿邮报报道，这个位于弗吉尼亚州的设施在太空时代之初曾作为水星计划的试验场发挥过作用，但在商业太空工业蓬勃发展的时候，它即将重塑自己。
在火箭实验室下大赌注。沃洛普斯的官员成功地拉拢了火箭实验室，他们预计火箭实验室可能每月发射一次。火箭实验室首席执行官彼得·贝克(Peter Beck)表示：我们运行了一个竞争过程。最终，沃洛普斯成为了赢家，因为它在附近有一个设施，公司可以在那里处理其有效载荷，准备发射卫星，然后快速将它们与火箭配对。(ColdWetDog提交)。
P120C电机第三次也是最后一次测试成功。欧洲航天局和阿丽亚尼集团报告说，在法属圭亚那进行的新型P120C固体火箭发动机的第三次静态射击试验取得成功。发动机将为织女星C火箭提供动力，也将作为阿丽亚娜6的侧装助推器，发射了130秒。
明年飞行...。在首次飞行前的最后一次测试中，发动机在阿丽亚娜6配置下运行，因为P120C将配备阿丽亚娜6的两种配置：阿丽亚娜62有两个助推器，阿丽亚娜64有四个助推器。测试是在10月7日进行的。织女星C和阿丽亚娜6号火箭都计划在明年首次亮相。
巴西计划在2022年推出本地发射器。巴西航天局主席周日表示，巴西计划在两年内发射国产轨道火箭。据Paralloic Arc报道，中国计划在2021年开始发动机测试，一年后推出。
我试着跑得快些..。巴西一直在与德国航空航天中心合作开发Viículo Lançado de Microssatélites 1(VLM-1)助推器和S-50发动机，它们将为火箭的前两级提供动力。三级VLM-1将能够向300公里的轨道发射150公斤。飞行将在位于巴西北大西洋海岸的阿尔坎塔拉发射中心进行。对我们来说，这似乎是一个咄咄逼人的日程安排。(Ken the Bin提交)。
ExoTerra将开发Launcher One上部阶段。据SpaceNews报道，科罗拉多州初创公司ExoTerra Resources周一宣布与美国国家航空航天局(NASA)签订了一份小型企业创新研究合同，将开发一种太阳能上级，以推动在维珍轨道(Virgin Orbit)的运载火箭一号(LauncherOne)上运行的超出低地球轨道的小型卫星。
太阳系探险家。该公司表示，有了ExoTerra的太阳能电力推进上级，LauncherOne的客户可以到达包括地球静止轨道、半月注入轨道、地月拉格朗日点和低月球轨道在内的目的地。维珍轨道公司表示，它已经看到渴望乘坐LauncherOne前往包括月球、火星、金星和小行星带在内的目的地的客户的强劲需求。(Ken the Bin提交)。
佛罗里达州的太空港可能会合并。据ARS报道，作为其未来范围计划的一部分，太空部队正在考虑的增加进入太空的想法之一是相当激进的-将其历史悠久的卡纳维拉尔角设施与美国国家航空航天局(NASA)的肯尼迪航天中心合并为一个太空港管理局。我们怎样才能到达一个类似的机场结构，在那里我们有一个军事基地机场和跑道，与商业跑道和机场并排坐着？太空部队的行动和通信总监德安娜·伯特少将说。
一个需要考虑的大主意..。太空部队的主要目标是执行其任务，包括快速可靠地进入太空。如果这意味着，就像它过去所做的那样，军方必须拥有和运营发射场的每一个方面，它会这样做的。但太空部队和伯特向更广泛的太空社区发出的信号是，如果有更好、更有效的方式做到这一点，减少新兴商业参与者的繁文缛节，他们愿意倾听。美国国家航空航天局(NASA)可能也愿意倾听。
俄罗斯发布了一款看起来很像猎鹰9号的火箭。这枚火箭助推器将由新的、尚未开发的燃烧甲烷的火箭发动机提供动力。据Ars报道，同样重要的是，俄罗斯首次寻求建造一个可重复使用的第一级。俄罗斯宇宙飞船的目标是在Amur上发射仅2200万美元的低价，广告称Amur能够向低地球轨道运送10.5吨。我们希望我们的火箭像卡拉什尼科夫冲锋枪一样可靠，亚历山大·布洛申科(Alexander Bloshenko)说，他是俄罗斯高级计划与科学公司(Roscosmos For Advanced Programs And Science)的执行董事。
要到2026年才能准备好。然而，Amur火箭设计中最引人注目的可能是，它与SpaceX&#39；猎鹰9号火箭的小型版本非常相似，后者可以将大约两倍的有效载荷送入轨道。SpaceX创始人埃隆·马斯克在推特上评论Amur火箭时表示，这是朝着正确方向迈出的一步，但他们的目标是到2026年实现完全可重复使用。更大的火箭对于字面上的规模经济也是有意义的。目标应该是将每可用吨轨道的成本降到最低，否则它充其量只能服务于利基市场。
note_en:
Welcome to Edition 3.19 of the Rocket Report! This week we were glad to see US rockets, led by the Antares and Falcon 9 vehicles, get back on track after a series of scrubs due to technical and weather issues. Meanwhile, the world's next launch is a big one: a Soyuz carrying three astronauts to the International Space Station next week.
 As always, we  welcome reader submissions, and if you don't want to miss an issue, please subscribe using the box below (the form will not appear on AMP-enabled versions of the site). Each report will include information on small-, medium-, and heavy-lift rockets as well as a quick look ahead at the next three launches on the calendar.
  Virgin Orbit's rocket comes at a high price. As Virgin Orbit drives toward its second launch attempt late in 2020, its expenses are adding up. Started in 2011 by Sir Richard Branson as an offshoot of his Virgin Galactic space business, Virgin Orbit has not revealed how much it has spent to date. But industry officials estimate it has expended between $500 million to $700 million developing LauncherOne and the infrastructure to support it.
 So much money for a relatively small rocket ...  In a new feature, Ars explores how this happened and investigates whether there is a path to profitability for the Long Beach, California-based company. Competitor Peter Beck of Rocket Lab is skeptical. "I don't see how that business case closes," he said in an interview. "How do you spend that much money and have a return on investment? And moreover, if you've spent that much money and you are where you are, maybe it's time to have a re-think."
 Space is becoming too crowded, Beck warns. CEO Beck (who features heavily in this week's report, we know) said that the sheer number of objects in space right now—a number that is growing quickly thanks in part to SpaceX's satellite Internet constellation, Starlink—is making it more difficult to find a clear path for rockets to launch new satellites. "This has a massive impact on the launch side,"  he told CNN Business. Rockets "have to try and weave their way up in between these constellations."
 Need for responsibility ... Beck said that he's not opposed to SpaceX's plans or massive satellite constellations in general. Starlink, once operational, could provide huge benefits to life on Earth by making Internet access available to the billions of people who still lack sufficient connectivity, he noted. But Beck said he is concerned about how rapidly he's seen traffic in space impact his own business. He's vowed to study the issue to help advance discussions on responsible space traffic management.
 The easiest way to keep up with Eric Berger's space reporting is to sign up for his newsletter, we'll collect his stories in your inbox.
 Wallops poised for growth? Born as a Navy air station during World War II, the Wallops Flight Facility has launched more than 16,000 rockets, most of them small sounding vehicles used for scientific research. But the Virginia-based facility, which at the dawn of the Space Age played a role as a test site for the Mercury program, is about to reinvent itself at a time when the commercial space industry is booming,  The Washington Post reports.
 Betting big on Rocket Lab ... Officials at Wallops successfully wooed Rocket Lab, which they anticipate may launch as much as once a month. "We ran a competitive process," Peter Beck, Rocket Lab's chief executive, said. In the end, Wallops was the winner because it had a facility nearby where the company could process its payloads, get the satellites ready for launch, and then mate them to a rocket quickly. (submitted by ColdWetDog)
 Third and final test of P120C motor a success. The European Space Agency and ArianeGroup  report that the third static-fire test of the new P120C solid rocket motor in French Guiana was a success. The motor, which will power both the Vega C rocket as well as serve as a side-mounted booster for the Ariane 6, fired for 130 seconds.
 Flying next year ... For this last test before its first flight, the motor operated in the Ariane 6 configuration, as the P120C will equip both configurations of Ariane 6: Ariane 62 has two boosters, and Ariane 64 has four boosters. The test took place on October 7. Both the Vega C and Ariane 6 rockets are scheduled to debut next year.
 Brazil planning native launcher for 2022. The president of the Brazilian Space Agency said on Sunday that Brazil plans to launch a domestically produced orbital rocket within two years. The country plans to start engine testing in 2021, before launching a year later,  Parabolic Arc reports.
 Trying to go fast ... Brazil has been working with the German Aerospace Center to develop the Veículo Lançador de Microssatélites 1 (VLM-1) booster and the S-50 engine that will power the rocket's first two stages. The three-stage VLM-1 will be capable of launching 150kg into a 300km orbit. Flights will be conducted from the Alcantara Launch Center, which is located on Brazil's northern Atlantic coast. It seems like an aggressive schedule to us. (submitted by Ken the Bin)
 ExoTerra to develop LauncherOne upper stage. Colorado startup ExoTerra Resources announced a NASA Small Business Innovative Research contract on Monday to develop a solar electric upper stage to boost small satellites traveling on Virgin Orbit's LauncherOne beyond low Earth orbit,  SpaceNews reports.
 Solar System explorer ... With ExoTerra's Solar Electric Propulsion Upper Stage, LauncherOne customers could reach destinations including geostationary orbit, translunar injection orbit, Earth-Moon Lagrange points, and low lunar orbit, the company said. Virgin Orbit said it has seen "robust demand from customers" eager to travel on LauncherOne to destinations including even the Moon, Mars, Venus, and the asteroid belt. (submitted by Ken the Bin)
  Florida spaceports may merge. As part of its "Range of the Future" initiative, one of the ideas the Space Force is considering to increase access to space is pretty radical—merging its historic Cape Canaveral facility with NASA's Kennedy Space Center under a single spaceport authority,  Ars reports. "How do we get to a similar airport structure where we have a military base airport and runway sitting side by side with the commercial runway and airport?" said the director of operations and communications for the Space Force, Maj. Gen. DeAnna Burt.
 A big idea to consider ... The primary goal of the Space Force is to execute its mission, which includes getting into space quickly and reliably. If that means, as it has done in the past, that the military has to own and operate every facet of a launch site, it will. But what the Space Force and Burt have signaled to the broader space community is that—if there's a better, more efficient way to do this, which cuts red tape for emerging commercial players—they're willing to listen. NASA may be willing to listen, too.
 Russia unveils a rocket that looks a lot like the Falcon 9. The "Amur" booster will be powered by new and as-yet-undeveloped rocket engines that burn methane. Just as significantly, for the first time, Russia is seeking to build a reusable first stage,  Ars reports. And Roscosmos is targeting a low price of just $22 million for a launch on Amur, which is advertised as being capable of delivering 10.5 tons to low Earth orbit. "We would like our rocket to be reliable, like a Kalashnikov assault rifle," said Alexander Bloshenko, executive director of Roscosmos for Advanced Programs and Science.
 Not ready until 2026 ... What is perhaps most striking about the Amur rocket design, however, is how much it resembles a smaller version of SpaceX's Falcon 9 rocket, which can lift about twice as much payload into orbit. Commenting on the Amur rocket on Twitter,  SpaceX founder Elon Musk said, "It's a step in the right direction, but they should really aim for full reusability by 2026. Larger rocket would also make sense for literal economies of scale. Goal should be to minimize cost per useful ton to orbit or it will at best serve a niche market."
title: 火箭报道：中国公布火箭大计划，SpaceX的“秘密”发射
title_en: Rocket Report: China unveils big rocket plans, SpaceX’s “secret” launch

note:
中国现在已经签署了一个大规模的全球联盟，以开发和公平地分发冠状病毒疫苗-这让美国没有加入该协议的情况得到了更大的缓解。
随着周五晚些时候宣布加入，中国加入了大约170个其他国家的行列，这个被称为Covax Facility的协定被称为Covax Facility。这项工作由世界卫生组织带头开展，并与防疫创新联盟(CEPI)和疫苗联盟GAVI共同领导。
Covax旨在汇集资源，帮助开发疫苗并将其推向全球市场。一旦达到这一点，联盟将帮助确保所有参与国都可以获得任何被证明成功的候选疫苗，无论它是在哪里开发的。它还将向低收入国家提供财政援助，使其能够获得疫苗。到目前为止，至少有77个高收入国家(包括中国)以及92个中低收入国家签约。
Covax的目标是在2021年底之前分发20亿剂针对大流行冠状病毒SARS-CoV-2的疫苗。尽管关于分配和获取的许多问题仍然存在，但各国领导人已经发布了疫苗分配的粗略两阶段计划，该计划将根据每个国家的人口规模开始，然后在第二阶段，从战略上权衡卫生需求和疾病在个别国家或地区的传播。
这一庞大计划的资金仍然不稳定，中国参与的条款也不清楚。但中国的加入可能会让前景变得光明。中国拥有世界第二大经济体，而且中国还在开发已经处于临床试验最后阶段的10种候选疫苗中的4种。
政府发言人华春莹在美联社报道的一份声明中表示：“我们正在采取这一具体步骤，以确保疫苗的公平分配，特别是对发展中国家的疫苗分配，并希望更多有能力的国家也加入并支持Covax。”
美国不在参与Covax的高收入国家之列。相反，它仍然与俄罗斯并驾齐驱，成为没有参与其中的最大经济体。在拒绝参与时，特朗普政府提到了在全球健康危机期间避开世界卫生组织(World Health Organization)的决定。专家们很快称这一举动是“短视的”和“弄巧成拙的”。
澳大利亚洛伊研究所的研究员娜塔莎·卡萨姆告诉美联社，美国的缺席对中国来说可能是一个福音。她说：“中国加入Covax有双重目的，一是填补美国在全球公共卫生方面留下的领导真空，二是为其人口确保未来的疫苗。”
尽管如此，GAVI首席执行官塞斯·伯克利(Seth Berkley)还是庆祝了中国的加入，他在周五早上的一条推文中写道：“很高兴欢迎中国加入#covax Facility！这项声明为我们确保未来的#COVID19疫苗公平分配的使命提供了更大的动力，因为在每个人都安全之前，没有人是安全的。#OneWorldProtected“
note_en:
China has now signed on to  a massive, global alliance to develop and equitably distribute a coronavirus vaccine—putting the United States’ absence from the pact into yet sharper relief.
 With its late entry announced Friday, China joins around 170 other countries in the pact, called  the COVAX Facility. The effort is being spearheaded by the World Health Organization and co-led along with the Coalition for Epidemic Preparedness Innovations (CEPI) and Gavi, the Vaccine Alliance.
 COVAX is designed to pool resources to help develop a vaccine and bring it to market globally. Once at that point, the alliance will help ensure all participating countries have access to whichever vaccine candidate(s) prove successful, regardless of where it was developed. It will also provide financial assistance to lower-income countries to access the vaccine. So far, at least 77 high-income countries (including China) have signed on, as well as 92 low- and middle-income countries.
 COVAX aims to distribute two billion doses of a vaccine against the pandemic coronavirus, SARS-CoV-2, by the end of 2021. Though many questions about distribution and access remain, leaders have released a rough two-phase plan for vaccine distribution, which will start based on each country’s population size and then, in the second phase, strategically weigh health needs and disease spread in individual countries or regions.
  The financing for the enormous plan is still shaky, and the terms of China’s involvement are unclear. But the entry of China may brighten the outlook. China has the second largest economy in the world, and it is also  developing four of the 10 vaccine candidates already in the final stages of clinical trials.
 “We are taking this concrete step to ensure equitable distribution of vaccines, especially to developing countries, and hope more capable countries will also join and support COVAX,” government spokesperson Hua Chunying said in  a statement reported by the Associated Press.
 The US is not among the high-income countries participating in COVAX. Instead, it remains in the ranks with Russia as the top economies not involved. In its refusal to participate, the Trump administration cited its decision to shun the World Health Organization amid the global health crisis. Experts were quick to call the move “ short-sighted” and “self-defeating.”
 The absence of the US may be a boon for China, Natasha Kassam, a research fellow at the Lowy Institute in Australia, told the AP. “China joining COVAX serves a dual purpose of filling the leadership vacuum left by the United States in terms of global public health, and in terms of securing a future vaccine for its population,” she said.
 Nevertheless, GAVI CEO Seth Berkley celebrated China’s entry, writing in  a Friday morning tweet: “Delighted to welcome China to the #COVAX Facility! This announcement gives even more momentum to our mission to ensure future #COVID19 vaccines are distributed equitably because no one is safe until everyone is safe. #OneWorldProtected”
title: 中国加入全球疫苗联盟，填补特朗普留下的“领导真空”
title_en: China joins global vaccine alliance, filling “leadership vacuum” left by Trump

note:
据“华尔街日报”昨天报道，美国电话电报公司(AT&amp；T)正计划在HBO、华纳兄弟和华纳传媒的其他部门裁员数千人，这是削减成本高达20%计划的一部分。
WarnerMedia过去被称为时代华纳公司(Time Warner Inc.)。在美国电话电报公司(AT&amp；T)于2018年收购这家娱乐公司之前。裁员和削减成本在美国电话电报公司(AT&amp；T)总体上并不是什么新鲜事，包括美国华纳传媒(WarnerMedia)。但自疫情爆发以来，华纳传媒受到的打击尤其严重。美国电话电报公司(AT&amp；T)8月份从华纳传媒裁员约600人，这是昨日披露的新裁员的前奏。“华尔街日报”写道：
据知情人士透露，美国电话电报公司(AT&amp；T&39；)旗下的WarnerMedia正在重组员工队伍，该公司正寻求削减高达20%的成本，因为冠状病毒疫情耗尽了电影票、有线电视订阅和电视广告的收入。
知情人士说，预计将在未来几周开始的这项改革，将导致华纳兄弟(Warner Bros)制片厂以及HBO、TBS和TNT等电视频道裁员数千人。
华纳传媒今年早些时候有近3万名员工。华纳传媒的一位发言人告诉路透社，我们不会讨论或证实任何关于裁员人数的猜测数字。这位发言人还说，裁员是8月份宣布的重组计划的一部分。
像娱乐业的其他人一样，我们也不能幸免于这场大流行的重大影响。WarnerMedia表示，这包括加快消费者行为的转变，特别是内容的观看方式。我们最近与员工分享，组织将进行重组，以应对这些变化并优先考虑增长机会，重点放在直接面向消费者上。我们正处于这一过程中，它将涉及增加对优先领域的投资，不幸的是，将减少对其他领域的投资。
在收购时代华纳(Time Warner)后不久，美国电话电报公司(AT&amp；T)表示，HBO需要大幅增加原创内容的制作，尽管该电视网曾凭借规模较小、质量较高的节目和电影阵容取得成功。HBO和特纳的老板在2019年的重组中离开了公司。
美国电话电报公司(AT&amp；T)将于本月晚些时候公布第三季度收益。在2020年第二季度的收益报告中，AT&Amp；T表示，HBO的收入为16亿美元，同比下降5.2%。华纳兄弟的收入为33亿美元，下降3.9%。特纳的收入为30亿美元，下降了12.4%。
美国电话电报公司(AT&amp；T)正在多条战线上苦苦挣扎。自2018年年中以来，该公司已经失去了700多万电视客户，到目前为止，它正在进行的出售DirecTV的尝试带来的出价约为美国电话电报公司(AT&amp；T)2015年收购这家卫星公司时出价的三分之一。在宽带方面，美国电话电报公司(AT&amp；T)停止向新客户销售DSL，并解雇了数千名网络技术人员，而不是扩大光纤到户网络。
正如“华尔街日报”所指出的，美国电话电报公司(AT&amp；T)的股价今年已下跌约28%，落后于康卡斯特(Comcast)等竞争对手，错过了股市创纪录的涨幅。
note_en:
AT&T is planning thousands of layoffs at HBO, Warner Bros., and other parts of WarnerMedia as part of a plan to cut costs by up to 20 percent, The Wall Street Journal  reported yesterday.
 WarnerMedia is what used to be called Time Warner Inc. before AT&T  purchased the entertainment company in 2018. Layoffs and cost cuts are  nothing new at  AT&T in general, including at  WarnerMedia. But WarnerMedia has taken a particularly big hit since the pandemic began.  AT&T laid off about 600 people from WarnerMedia in August, a prelude to the new cuts revealed yesterday. The Journal wrote:
 AT&T's WarnerMedia is restructuring its workforce as it seeks to reduce costs by as much as 20 percent as the coronavirus pandemic drains income from movie tickets, cable subscriptions and television ads, according to people familiar with the matter.
 The overhaul, which is expected to begin in the coming weeks, would result in thousands of layoffs across Warner Bros. studios and TV channels like HBO, TBS and TNT, the people said.
  WarnerMedia had nearly 30,000 employees earlier this year. A WarnerMedia spokesperson told Ars that "we are not discussing or confirming any speculative numbers" regarding how many jobs will be cut. The job reductions are part of the restructuring that was announced in August, the spokesperson also said.
 "Like the rest of the entertainment industry, we have not been immune to the significant impact of the pandemic. That includes an acceleration in shifting consumer behavior, especially in the way content is being viewed," WarnerMedia said. "We shared with our employees recently that the organization will be restructured to respond to those changes and prioritize growth opportunities, with an emphasis on direct-to-consumer. We are in the midst of that process and it will involve increased investments in priority areas and, unfortunately, reductions in others."
 Shortly after buying Time Warner, AT&T said that HBO needed to  ramp up production of original content despite the network's history of succeeding with a smaller, high-quality lineup of shows and movies. The HBO and Turner bosses  left the company amid a reorganization in 2019.
  AT&T will report third-quarter earnings later this month. In its  Q2 2020 earnings report, AT&T said that HBO revenue was $1.6 billion, down 5.2 percent year over year. Warner Bros. revenue was $3.3 billion, down 3.9 percent. Turner revenue was $3 billion, down 12.4 percent.
  AT&T is struggling on multiple fronts. It has lost over 7 million TV customers since mid-2018, and its ongoing  attempt to sell DirecTV has so far brought in bids valued at about one-third of what AT&T paid for the satellite company in 2015. On the broadband front, AT&T  stopped selling DSL to new customers and  laid off thousands of network technicians  instead of expanding its fiber-to-the-home network.
 As the Journal noted, "AT&T shares have fallen about 28 percent this year, lagging behind rivals like Comcast and missing out on the stock market's record run."
title: 美国电话电报公司(AT&T)计划在HBO、华纳兄弟(Warner Bros)和华纳媒体(WarnerMedia)其他部门裁员数千人
title_en: AT&T plans thousands of layoffs at HBO, Warner Bros., rest of WarnerMedia

note:
微软(Microsoft)游戏流媒体服务Project XCloud在某些Xbox Game Pass订阅计划中被打包为额外奖励，它可能终于有了一条在苹果(Apple)的一系列iOS设备上工作的途径--很久之前，这两家科技巨头之间的公开争吵让这一可能性受到了质疑。
这一消息来自Business Insider的一份报告，该报告称，微软周三的内部会议包括Xbox首席执行官菲尔·斯宾塞(Phil Spencer)的信任投票。据报道，斯宾塞表示，2021年将把其流媒体Project XCloud游戏内容放到iOS设备上。&#34；我们绝对会在iOS上结束。&#34；据报道，斯宾塞曾表示要将其流媒体Project XCloud游戏内容放到iOS设备上。&#34；
此前，苹果曾将Project XCloud(后来被整合到Xbox Game Pass应用程序中)和谷歌体育场(Google Stadia)的现有版本都斥为iOS应用程序。他们的公开声明取决于根据App Store的指导方针审查订阅中包含的游戏，尽管这个问题也可能源于Xbox和Stadia提供的应用内购买。最终，苹果公司修改了对这类应用程序的立场，但这项繁琐的每一款游戏的审批提案都有其令人头疼的问题，而不是一条通向简单订阅服务的明确道路(你可以在Netflix和亚马逊视频(Amazon Video)等流行的iOS媒体应用程序中找到这种情况)。
大约一个月后，亚马逊宣布推出自己的游戏流媒体服务亚马逊露娜，该公司表示，这项服务将无法在iOS设备上运行。多么?。亚马逊的计划将跳过App Store，转而在iOS和Safari应用中使用渐进式网络应用，苹果不能以同样的方式禁止这一应用。
不出所料，本周的微软会议呼吁建立一个类似的基于网络的解决方案，让XCloud在iOS设备上运行。这可能会将XCloud的覆盖范围扩大到更多的设备，果不其然，Verge后来证实，同一次会议包括呼吁在2021年让XCloud在Windows10设备上运行。
然而，这些报告没有澄清的是，当XCloud从一款内置应用转变为通过标准网络浏览器路由调用的应用时，XCloud业界领先的延迟指标将会发生什么变化。XCloud在从服务器流媒体紧张的视频游戏方面发挥了重大作用。但是，这些报告没有澄清的是，XCloud行业领先的延迟指标在从服务器流媒体视频游戏方面产生了重大影响。根据我们对XCloud在Android上的测试，我们不确定微软是否已经围绕一个基于网络的应用程序构建了一个简单的包装-因此，XCloud团队可能还有一些工作要做，才能在这个星球上最大的操作系统之一上快速上手。
note_en:
Project xCloud, the Microsoft game-streaming service that  comes packed as a bonus in certain Xbox Game Pass subscription plans, may finally have a path to working on Apple's range of iOS devices—well after a public row between the tech giants that put the possibility into question.
 The news comes from  a report by Business Insider, which claimed that an internal Microsoft meeting on Wednesday included a vote of confidence from Xbox chief Phil Spencer. "We absolutely will end up on iOS," Spencer reportedly said about getting its streamed Project xCloud game content onto iOS devices in "2021."
  Previously,  Apple shot down existing versions of both Project xCloud (which has since been rolled into the "Xbox Game Pass" app) and Google Stadia as iOS apps. Their public statements hinged on "reviewing" the games included in the subscription against App Store guidelines, though the issue could also stem from in-app purchases within both Xbox and Stadia's offerings. Eventually,  Apple offered a revised stance on such apps, but this onerous "approval for every separate game" proposal comes with its own headaches, as opposed to a clear path toward a simple subscription service (as you'll find in popular iOS media apps like Netflix and  Amazon Video).
 Roughly one month later, Amazon announced its own game-streaming service,  Amazon Luna, which the company said  would work on iOS devices. How? Amazon's plan will skip the App Store and operate instead with a "progressive Web application" inside of iOS' Safari app, which Apple cannot prohibit in the same way.
 Unsurprisingly, this week's Microsoft meeting included a call to build a similar Web-based solution for getting xCloud running on iOS devices. That would likely broaden xCloud's reach to even more devices, and sure enough,  The Verge later confirmed that the same meeting included a call to have xCloud running on Windows 10 devices in 2021.
 What these reports don't clarify, however, is how xCloud's industry-leading latency measures, which make a serious difference in streaming twitchy video games from a server, will change when moving from a built-in app to one that routes its calls through a standard Web browser. Based on  our tests of xCloud on Android, we're not sure that Microsoft has built a simple wrapper around a Web-based app—and thus the xCloud team probably has some work ahead of them to get up to speed on one of the planet's biggest operating systems.
title: 报告：Xbox游戏流媒体将于2021年使用网络解决方案登陆iOS
title_en: Report: Xbox game streaming will land on iOS in 2021 using Web workaround

note:
彭博社(Bloomberg)的一份最新报告详细介绍了谷歌从YouTube中榨取更多收入的新计划，YouTube已经是一项年收入150亿美元的业务。谷歌显然想把YouTube变成一个购物目的地，在那里，观众可以观看产品拆箱、化妆或烹饪视频，并立即通过YouTube直接购买特色产品。
报告称，YouTube目前正在有限的几个视频频道上测试这些功能，目标是将YouTube上的大量视频转化为观众可以细读、点击和直接购买的大量项目。该公司正在要求创作者通过一款新软件来标记视频中出现的项目，该软件可以用于分析和购买。显然，YouTube测试购物功能已经有一段时间了。彭博社(Bloomberg)的报告称，去年年底，YouTube开始测试类似的Shopify集成，创作者可以在视频下方的数字旋转木马上列出多达12件待售商品。Shopify是一家交钥匙在线商店提供商。
目前，这一切都是一次测试，但彭博社得到了YouTube发言人的证实，该公司正在研究该网站的电子商务选择。谷歌首席执行官桑达尔·皮查伊实际上将YouTube Shopping的想法放在了该公司2020年第一季度的收益上，他说，当你考虑到拆箱和产品评论等事情时，这些也是进行交易的天然场所。我刚才提到了我们现在正在做的所有商业工作。所有这一切，我期待着这些集成进入YouTube，并且工作得更好。
谷歌一直渴望新的非广告收入来源，如今，YouTubers兜售亚马逊会员链接的情况并不少见，在那里，他们从任何点击量销售中获得回扣。谷歌的计划是去掉中间人，直接把这些购买按钮放在YouTube上，这样谷歌就可以从销售额中分得一杯羹。
note_en:
A fresh report from  Bloomberg details Google's new plan to squeeze even more revenue out of YouTube, which is already a $15 billion-a-year business. Google apparently wants to turn YouTube into a shopping destination, where viewers can watch things like product unboxings, makeup, or cooking videos and immediately buy featured products directly through YouTube.
 The report says YouTube is currently "testing these features with a limited number of video channels" and "[t]he goal is to convert YouTube's bounty of videos into a vast catalog of items that viewers can peruse, click on, and buy directly." The company is asking creators to tag items featured in videos through a new piece of software that can be used for analytics and purchasing. Apparently, YouTube has been testing shopping features for a while now. Bloomberg's report says, "Late last year, YouTube began testing a similar Shopify integration for creators who can list as many as 12 items for sale on a digital carousel below their videos, according to the company." Shopify is a turnkey online store provider.
 For now, this is all a test, but Bloomberg got a YouTube spokesperson to confirm that the company is looking into e-commerce options for the site. Google CEO Sundar Pichai actually pitched the idea of YouTube Shopping on the company's Q1 2020 earnings, saying, "When you think about things like unboxing and product reviews, those are a natural home for transactions as well. I earlier mentioned about all the work we are doing now on commerce. All of that, I'm looking forward to those integrations coming into YouTube and working better as well."
 Google is always hungry for new, non-advertising revenue streams, and today it's not uncommon to see YouTubers hawking Amazon affiliate links, where they get a kickback from any click-through sales. Google's plan would cut out the middle man and stick those buy buttons directly on YouTube, where it would presumably get a cut of sales.
title: 谷歌希望将YouTube变成具有新购物功能的QVC
title_en: Google wants to turn YouTube into QVC with new shopping features
